content: I need to data mine all song lyrics for these two hispanic artists: Los tigres del norte, Los tucanes de tijuana. the goal is to analyse the content of the lyrics over time and attempt to match it with political topics to understand if there is a correlation of topics. you can use python for data mining, data fixing and a simple one pager html for data visualization. it's for a scientific paper so visualization and data structure (with sources) is vital. store data locally in json or csv formats

Entered plan mode. Clarified: lyrics source (Genius API + lyricsgenius), political axes (all four: drug war MX, immigration US-MX, elections MX, US presidency), NLP method (all three — keywords, BERTopic, hybrid+sentiment — for comparison), visualization (interactive dashboard with filters and per-song drill-down).
yes and use auto mode, log the plan into the folder

Built full project scaffold: directory structure, requirements.txt, .env.example, .gitignore, CLAUDE.md. Created 4 Spanish lexicons (migracion, narco, politica_mx, politica_us). Curated 65 political events CSV with source URLs across all 4 axes (1980–2025). Wrote all 8 Python pipeline scripts (scrape_genius, clean_lyrics, build_events, analyze_keywords, analyze_bertopic, analyze_hybrid, correlate, export_dashboard). Built interactive dashboard (index.html + app.js) with 5 panels, Plotly.js, filters, and song drill-down modal.
do this for me, until the scrap script is run. i'll manually add the api token before you can run it

Set up Python venv. Hit SSL cert issue with pyenv Python — fixed by installing 'truststore'. Fixed lyricsgenius API breaking changes (verbose param removed, sort='year' removed, Song.id → to_dict()['id'], release_date_components schema). Discovered Genius public search API (/api/search) blocked by Cloudflare — hardcoded known artist IDs (Los Tigres: 68345, Los Tucanes: 357527) to use authenticated API directly. Validated political_events.csv (65 events, all sourced, all dates parse). Prompted user to add Genius API token to .env.
i found the request header saying this: [full browser Cookie header pasted]
note: User added GENIUS_BROWSER_COOKIE to .env — contains cf_clearance and session cookies for bypassing Cloudflare on genius.com lyrics pages.

content: Verified browser cookie bypasses Cloudflare (HTTP 200). Matched User-Agent to Safari (same as cookie). Rebuilt scraper to: (1) fetch all song metadata via Genius REST API, (2) scrape lyrics from genius.com using browser session cookie. Fixed CSV quoting bug in political_events.csv. Ran full scrape: 558 Los Tigres del Norte songs, 317 Los Tucanes de Tijuana songs (875 total). 553 + 314 = 867 lyrics successfully fetched. Ran clean_lyrics.py (871 songs after dedup, 851 Spanish). Ran analyze_keywords.py: 45% narco prevalence, 36% migration prevalence across corpus. Ran correlate.py: first significant finding — Los Tucanes migration topic correlates with US presidential terms (r=0.28, p=0.032). Exported dashboard/data.json. Dashboard functional at localhost:8000.

BLOCKER: Methods 2 (BERTopic) and 3 (pysentimiento) both download models from huggingface.co. Novo Nordisk Zscaler proxy blocks huggingface.co under AI_ML_WEBUSE_DENIED policy. These two methods cannot run on this network. Method 1 (keyword analysis) is fully complete. Instructions left for running Methods 2+3 on a different network.
no it's all good for now. for now i want the html to also contain pages that can review and display all the lyric data including the meta data

Rebuilt dashboard as a 3-page SPA: (1) Analysis — original 5-panel charts view; (2) Lyrics Browser — split-pane with searchable/filterable list of all 871 songs on the left, full song detail panel on the right showing all metadata fields, keyword hit breakdown per lexicon, political context cards ±2 years, and full lyrics text; (3) Political Events — full searchable/sortable/filterable table of all 65 curated events with axis color-coding, descriptions, types, and source links. Updated export_dashboard.py to include full lyrics in data.json (~2MB payload).
store this session in prompts/ so we can go through correct sequence of the prompting process or even display it later on the html
